title:
iTero
Scan Experience
subtitle: Redesigning the clinical scanning workflow to improve speed, clarity, and decision-making in real treatment conditions.

slides.title: iTero Clinical Workflow
slides.description: Redesigning the experience from RX to Scan to View into a continuous system that supports real-time decision making during patient treatment.

slides.label: Context
slides.title: A system used during live treatment
slides.content: iTero is used while the patient is in the chair. The workflow moves through three connected phases — RX (setup), Scan (capture), and View (validation). Decisions in each phase directly affect the next.

slides.label: Problem
slides.title: When system growth breaks flow
slides.content: Over time, the system evolved feature by feature without a unified interaction logic. RX, Scan, and View became disconnected, creating friction across the workflow.
issues: RX blocks users before they can start scanning
Procedure and tooth selection require mental effort
Scanning is interrupted by tool interaction
Tool feedback is unclear and requires interpretation
Validation is not explicit, leading to repeated checks
Users lose flow and confidence during the process

slides.label: Research
slides.title: From system issues to real behavior
slides.content: To understand the problem , we observed clinicians during live sessions and conducted interviews.

quotes.text: The patient popup interrupts me before I even start.
quotes.author: Dr. Yael Levi

quotes.text: I change the procedure multiple times before scanning.
quotes.author: Dr. Amir Cohen

quotes.text: If I look for tools, I lose my rhythm completely.
quotes.author: Dr. Noa Ben-David

quotes.text: I don’t trust the result immediately.
quotes.author: Dr. Efrat Mizrahi

quotes.text: I keep checking even after finishing.
quotes.author: Dr. Daniel Katz

quotes.text: Tooth selection is confusing to map.
quotes.author: Dr. Lior Haddad

slides.label: Goals
slides.title: Translating friction into measurable improvements

goals.title: Enable immediate case start
goals.description: Remove blocking steps in RX and reduce setup time

goals.title: Support continuous scanning flow
goals.description: Minimize interruptions and tool-related friction during scan

goals.title: Improve clarity of tools and states
goals.description: Make actions and outcomes instantly understandable

goals.title: Strengthen validation confidence
goals.description: Reduce need for repeated checks in View

kpis: Time to first scan action (RX → Scan start)

slides.label: Process
slides.title: From system analysis to real-world validation

steps.title: Map the system
steps.description: Analyzed the full journey (RX → Scan → View) to understand how flows, tools, and decisions are connected

steps.title: Audit real friction
steps.description: Identified where users hesitate, stop, or repeat actions by observing real clinical behavior

steps.title: Redefine interaction logic
steps.description: Restructured the experience from isolated screens into a continuous, state-driven system

steps.title: Prototype with Cursor
steps.description: Built interactive prototypes to simulate real scanning workflows and test behavior, not just UI

steps.title: Test & iterate
steps.description: Validated decisions with clinicians and refined flows based on real usage patterns

slides.label: RX Entry Flow
slides.title: From blocking setup to continuous entry
slides.beforeDescription: The system forced users into a rigid sequence before allowing them to start scanning.
slides.afterDescription: The redesigned flow removes blocking steps and enables immediate action.

slides.label: Procedure Selection
slides.title: From abstract selection to clear decisions
slides.beforeDescription: Users relied on dropdowns disconnected from the clinical context.
slides.afterDescription: Visual selection reduces cognitive effort and speeds decisions.

slides.label: Scan Flow
slides.title: From interruption to continuity
slides.beforeDescription: Users needed to stop scanning to interact with UI elements.
slides.afterDescription: The system now supports continuous scanning without interruption.

slides.label: Tools
slides.title: From manual control to guided interaction
slides.beforeDescription: Tools required manual adjustments and interpretation.
slides.afterDescription: Tools now guide decisions with clear feedback.

slides.label: Scan Flexibility
slides.title: Adding flexibility without breaking flow
slides.content: Tabs were introduced to allow switching between scan contexts without leaving the main interaction. This provides flexibility while maintaining continuity.

slides.label: Validation
slides.title: From uncertainty to confidence
slides.beforeDescription: Users relied on repeated checks to validate results.
slides.afterDescription: The system now clearly communicates completion and quality.

slides.label: Multi-layer
slides.title: Making differences visible
slides.content: The multi-layer panel allows users to overlay scans and adjust opacity, turning comparison into a visual, immediate action instead of a mental task.

slides.label: Prototyping
slides.title: Testing real behavior, not just UI
slides.content: We built interactive prototypes using Cursor to simulate real workflows. This allowed us to test decisions under realistic conditions and iterate quickly.

outcomes.title: Better clarity
outcomes.description: Improved understanding of tools and states
